note: Highest single-year total at the time. COVID-19 outbreaks in detention facilities account for multiple deaths. ACLU documented ICE refusing to release medically vulnerable detainees despite risk.

New all-time record for ICE deaths in a single fiscal year. Detention Watch Network counts 31 for calendar year 2025. Advocates attribute to overcrowding, inadequate medical staffing, and rapid detention expansion under Trump second term.

category: Deaths in ICE Custody (FY2026, through Apr 18, 2026)
note: FY2026 total of 29 deaths (Oct 2025–Apr 2026) breaks the 22-year-old record of 28 set in FY2004. Calendar year 2026 total of 17 includes Aled Damien Carbonell-Betancourt, 27, Cuban national found unresponsive at Miami federal detention center on April 18, presumed suicide. ICE released delayed 90-day reports for 4 deaths — Victor Manuel Diaz, Heber Sanchez Dominguez, Parady La, Luis Nunez Caceres — missing congressional reporting deadlines. Only 15 of 49 deaths since January 2025 received required 48-hour interim notices. ICE stopped paying for third-party medical treatment Oct 3, 2025. Fort Bliss Camp East Montana death ruled homicide.

source: HRW — Deaths in ICE Custody Report (2018); ICE.gov historical data
note: Human Rights Watch reported approximately 180 deaths in ICE custody between FY2003 and FY2017, averaging roughly 12 per year. Pre-2018 data is less systematic; mandatory 90-day death reviews not required until FY2018 appropriations.

First year of mandatory death review reporting under Congressional requirement. Seven deaths documented; ICE published 90-day reviews as required. Includes Gourgen Mirimanian (Adelanto) and Huy Chi Tran.

Eight deaths in FY2019. Includes Simratpal Singh (Sikh activist detained at Sacramento), whose case drew significant media attention. DHS OIG investigated multiple deaths for medical care deficiencies.

Five deaths in Biden's first fiscal year — reduction from the COVID-era FY2020 high. Still includes preventable deaths from inadequate medical care. ACLU continued litigation over conditions at Adelanto and Stewart facilities.

Spike to 12 deaths in FY2024 under Biden, ahead of detention population growth. Notable deaths include Ousmane Ba (Senegal) whose family alleged medical neglect at Otay Mesa detention center. Congressional investigation requested.

category: Children Separated from Parents (2018 Family Separation Crisis)
source: DHS IG; Ms. L v. ICE Court Record; HHS
note: Over 2,700 children separated from parents under zero tolerance policy (May–June 2018); some estimates reach 4,000+ when accounting for pre-zero-tolerance separations. Hundreds of families not reunified years later. Long-term psychological trauma documented by pediatric and mental health experts.

killed: Multiple (COVID-linked deaths included in FY2020 count)
source: ICE.gov COVID data; ACLU Nationwide Survey 2020
note: ACLU documented over 8,000 confirmed COVID-19 cases among ICE detainees by mid-2020. ICE refused to release medically vulnerable detainees despite emergency litigation. Multiple outbreak facilities — Otay Mesa, Adelanto, Bluebonnet — documented as COVID hotspots. Exact COVID-attributed death count disputed.
